• Complete the FCO Record with:

– The date you added the Notice
– Your signature

Item Part Values
Overview
2.1 Overview
This chapter gives the information about the Treatment Control System (TCS) software items and
parts.
2.1.1 Software items
To control and monitor the digital accelerator, the TCS software is divided into small packages
known as items. All of the systems and subsystems of the machine, and many individual
components, have related items.
There are three main types of item:
• Receive items - which collect data from the digital accelerator and are connected to the
RTUs in the RHCA, HTCA and ICCA
• Transmit items - which send data to the digital accelerator and are connected to the RTUs
in the RHCA, HTCA and ICCA
• Control items - which take data from receive items and pass data to transmit items.
Each item has a unique identifying number: for example, i15, i200 and so on. At present, items i0 to
i835 are assigned to the digital accelerator and the Precise Table, and items i2000 to i2271 are
assigned to the MultiLeaf Collimator and Beam Modulator™. However, it should be noted that
individual item numbers, the function of an item, and the range of item numbers are subject to
change as equipment is developed.
Detailed information on all software items is given in Section2.1.5.
2.1.2 Parts
Each software item have many different parameters known as parts, which are arranged in a
standard format. The parts change data values to actual MU (volts, amps, degrees, millimeters and
so on). Conceptually, the parts can be considered as a database of several thousand parameters that
are accessed by the software items at need. However, not all parts are used by every software item.
Detailed information on the parts is given in Section2.5 for the digital accelerator and Precise
Table, in Section2.5 for the MultiLeaf Collimator, and in Section2.6 for the Beam Modulator™.
2.1.3 Calibration blocks
The active, dynamic information about the digital accelerator is shown in a database. This database
has the machine-specific settings (such as the electron gun current), and data that is specific to the
selected energy.
The energy-specific information is shown in groups of data known as calibration blocks. The
calibration block for the applicable energy is read by the software when the energy is selected, and is
updated with new values when the Save Energy Cal. Blocks command is used.
2.1.4 Viewing and adjusting items and parts
Three procedures to see and, if necessary, adjust the values of software items and parts in Integrity™:
• Service pages
• Edit machine item part
• Display machine item.

Item Part Values
Overview
2.1.4.1 Service pages
The Service page(s) of Integrity™ lets you see and adjust the values of the software items used by the
applicable system, subsystem or function.
1 To get access to the service pages, click the Display Service Pages icon or press
<Shift>+<F6>).
The Display Service Pages dialog opens.
2 To choose a service page, click the Load button or press <Shift>+<F6>).
The Service Page Selection panel opens.
3 In the Pages Available list, click the name of the service page that you want to load, then click
OK.
The selected service page opens.
2.1.4.2 Edit machine item part
The Edit Machine Item Part function lets you see the software items that do not appear on a
Integrity™ service page.
1 To open the Edit Machine Item Part panel, click the Edit Machine Item Part icon or press
<Shift>+<F9>).
2 Type the item number that you want to view into a data field, or use a menu button to choose
the item name from a drop-down list of items.
2.1.4.3 Display machine item
1 To see the parts in an item, click the Display Machine Item icon or press <Shift>+<F6>).
The Display Machine Item dialog opens, showing the present values of all parts in the selected
item.
For detailed instructions on using Integrity™ R1.x in Service mode, see the applicable Integrity™ R1.x
Instructions for Use - Service Mode User Manual.
2.1.5 Item Descriptions
The information about each item is given in a separate table as follows:
Item name The name of the item
Description A brief description of the item
Data type The data type of the item (if applicable)
Range The permissible range of the item (if applicable)
Resolution The bit resolution of the item (if applicable)
Units The unit that the value of the item represents (if applicable)
Service page(s) The page(s) on which the item appears in Integrity™ in Service Mode (if
applicable)
System(s) Cross reference to the digital accelerator system(s) or subsystem(s) in which
the item is used (if applicable)
See [Ref: 14] Cross reference to the system diagram number(s) on which the item appears
(if applicable)

Item Part Values
Overview
The values for data type, range, resolution, and units are the values for part 1 and part 4 of the item.
If the values such as range, resolution, and units are shown in Resolution row (in brackets), then all
are applicable to the stated part number.
The data type of the item can be one of the following:
Integer 16-bit signed, -32767 to +32767
Word 16-bit unsigned, 0 to 65535
Byte 8-bit unsigned, 0 to 255
Real Real numbers with one or two decimal place precision
Boolean Bit 0 indicates the boolean state: 1 = True, 0 = False
True: odd numbers or False: even numbers
Item number The item contains another item number
container
Void No data type
Note: If a table of an item number appears again (same item number), then this means that the item was
used under a different description for a different Integrity™ software release which will be indicated on
the first line.

2.2 Digital Accelerator items
This section provides detailed information about all the software items used by the Digital
Accelerator.
2.2.1 Items 0 to 99
2.2.1.1 i0 - Unknown item
Item name Unknown Item
Description Message text used for reporting an inhibit when the fault is not associated
with a particular system or component
Data type Void
Range Not available
Resolution Not available
Units None
Service page(s) Edit Machine Item Part
System(s) Not available
See Not available
2.2.1.2 i1 - Calibration block identification
Item name Cal blk ID
Description This item identifies the energy calibration block that is currently set on the
digital accelerator.
The last two digits of the item value correspond to the energy code.
If a wedge is prescribed, an offset value of 20 is applied to the identities of
X-ray calibration blocks.
Data type Integer
Range 5000 to 5999
Resolution Integer
Units None
Service page(s) Edit Machine Item Part
System(s) Not available
See Not available

2.2.1.5 i4 - Execute automatic
Item name Execn.Auto
Description This item is selected when a change in multi-segment beam from one
segment to the next is made with or without operator intervention.
In the latter condition, an automatic interrupt occurs after the next segment
is received. The operator executes the next segment by pressing the <START>
button.
Data type Boolean
Range 0 to 2
Resolution Integer
Units None

2.2.1.36 i35 - Segment dose channel 1 monitor
Item name Seg MU 1
Description This item sets the segment dose per segment for dose channel 1. The
actual value returned is the number of MU given per segment.
The sum of the segment MU for all segments of a beam must be less
than the total MU for that beam - otherwise an abnormal termination
results.
i683 allows this item to display the generated radiation and read on
channel 1.
Data type Real
Range 0.0MU to 1023.0MU
Resolution 0.1MU
Units MU
Service page(s) Dose Calibration, Dosimetry

2.2.1.37 i36 - Segment dose channel 2 monitor
Item name Seg MU 2
Description This item sets the segment dose backup per segment for dose channel 2.
The actual value returned is the number of MU given per segment.
The segment MU set for channel 2 must exceed the set value for channel
1 to avoid abnormal termination.
If no value for channel 2 is entered, the system calculates the value from
channel 1 set by adding 10 MU.
i684 allows this item to display the generated radiation and read on
channel 1.
Data type Real
Range 0.0MU to 1023.0MU
Resolution 0.1MU
Units MU
Service page(s) Dose Calibration, Dosimetry
System(s) Dosimetry [Ref: 9]

2.2.1.47 i46 - Inhibit reason 1
Item name Inhibit 1
Description One of a group of 12 items (i46 to i57) which show any reason why an
increase in the readiness state of the digital accelerator may not occur.
An increase in readiness is, for example, the progression from the
Preparatory state to the Ready state, or the transition from System on to
Closed.
The reasons are displayed in the order detected except that, if the parent of a
violated interlock is also violated, the daughters are not shown. For example,
it is not necessary to show that the bending current is out of limits if the LT
circuit breaker has tripped.
Data type Item number container
Range 0 to maximum item number, plus NULL
Resolution Integer
Units None
Service page(s) Reason Items

Item Part Values
Digital Accelerator items
2.2.2.97 i196 - Pressure switch low
Item name Press.sw.L
Description This item reads the state of the low pressure switch in the return line to the
cooling pump and indicates when cooling water pressure is too low. It warns
that the water pressure is less than 0.7bar (10psi) and therefore the low
pressure switch has opened. At atmospheric pressure, the switch contacts are
normally closed.
In response, the TCC issues a warning (inhibit) only.
Data type Boolean
Range 0 Water pressure OK 1 Water pressure low
Resolution Integer
Units None
Service page(s) Modulator
System(s) Cooling, Gas and Vacuum [Ref: 4]
See [Ref: 14] 4513 330 7044 - sheet 1
2.2.2.98 i197 - Pressure switch high
Item name Press.sw.H
Description This item reads the state of the high pressure switch in the return line to the
cooling pump and indicates when cooling water pressure is too high. It warns
that the water pressure exceeds 1bar (15 psi) and therefore the high pressure
switch has opened. At atmospheric pressure, the switch contacts are normally
closed.
In response, the TCC issues a warning (inhibit) only.
Data type Boolean
Range 0 Water pressure OK 1 Water pressure high
Resolution Integer
Units None
Service page(s) Modulator
System(s) Cooling, Gas and Vacuum [Ref: 4]
See [Ref: 14] 4513 330 7044 - sheet 1
Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator 1015122 02

4095–current dose_ref actualdose
or:
displayed dose 4095–current dose_ref
new dose_ref = 4095–---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
actual dose

Description This item sets the segment dose per segment for dose channel 1. The value
will be set to the segment hardware counter.
The sum of the segment MU for all segments of a beam must be less than the
total MU for that beam - otherwise an abnormal termination will result.

Resolution 0.1MU
Units MU

2.2.4.66 i365 - Prescribed segment dose for channel 1
Item name Sg MU2 set
Description This item sets the segment dose backup per segment for dose channel 2. The
value will be set to the segment hardware counter.
The segment MU set for channel 2 must exceed the set value for channel 1 to
avoid abnormal termination.
If no value for channel 2 is entered, the TCC calculates the value from channel
1 set by adding 10MU.
Data type Not available
Range 0.0MU to 1023.0MU
Resolution 0.1MU
Units MU

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
2.3 Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
This section explains the purpose of significant parts used by the software items in the digital
accelerator. It is applicable for Desktop Pro™ R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x, some parts may not exist for
earlier software releases but may be applicable to later software releases.
2.3.1 Parts 0 to 99
2.3.1.1 Part 1 - Setting value
This part contains the requested or required value.
2.3.1.2 Part 2 - Tolerance high
This part sets the high limit of the value in part 1.
2.3.1.3 Part 3 - Exception code
This part contains the exception code. For information on Exception codes see ChapterAppendix
A.
2.3.1.4 Part 4 - Actual value
This part is the read back part in calibrated units. The value of part 4 is obtained by multiplying the
valid value by the calibration gain divided by 2048. Thus:
part 4 = part 110 × (part 11/2048)
2.3.1.5 Part 5 - Change queue
This part defines default change queue.
2.3.1.6 Part 6 - Tolerance low
This part sets the low limit of the value in part 1.
2.3.1.7 Part 10 - Calibration offset
This part is the offset value that is applied to part 111 during calibration.
2.3.1.8 Part 11 - Calibration gain
This part is the gain value that is applied to part 110 during calibration.
2.3.1.9 Part 12 - Calibration inverse
This part is the inverse gain value that is applied to limits to obtain raw limits.
2.3.1.10 Part 13 - Access pointer
This part is the index to all fac 1 arrays and can be used together with the control pointer access to
gain access to any variable in the system.
Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator 1015122 02
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes
Copyright ©2013 Elekta Limited. All rights reserved. PPaaggee 229911

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
2.3.1.11 Part 14 - Access mask
This part is used to distinguish between incoming raw analog and digital data.
A value of -1 denotes analog data.
A value between 0 and 65535 denotes digital data.
2.3.1.12 Part 15 - Action mask
This part determines the action to be taken when a limit is violated. Table2.30 shows the
permitted values of part 15.
Table2.30 Part 15 - Values of action mask
Value Action
0 Machine shut down by FAC.1
1 Emergency shutdown. Switch Off system contactor CON-A
2 Switch off magnetron and gun LT supply for reason such as temperature limit
exceeded.
3 Switch Off movements contactor CON-B
4 Terminate FACILITY 2 due to the violation of a limit
5 Interrupt due to the violation of a limit
6 Not used
7 Not used
8 Inhibit only. A start is inhibited but, during irradiation, only a warning is
given
9 Warning only. To be used in Service mode. Automatically reset on entering
Clinical mode
10 Warning only while HT is on
11 Ignore this error
12 Interrupt in radiation state other than INTERSEGMENT
13 Terminate in radiation state other than INTERSEGMENT
14 Fault reset
15 End of segment
16 Inhibit start
17 Not used
18 Cause end of segment in MOVE ONLY mode
19 Switch off LT even if the system state is ‘System On’ state.
20 Terminate, except if currently interrupted
2.3.1.13 Part 16 - Parent interlock
This part defines hierarchy of interlocks. If the item in this part is also an inhibit then this item will
not be displayed.

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
2.3.1.14 Part 17 - Default frequency
This part defines default frequency queue.
2.3.1.15 Part 18 - Item type
This part defines how an item is treated.
The lower 5 bits define 32 different item types being subdivisions of transmit items, receive items
and process items to select the type of processing required per item.
The upper 11 bits define a number of attributes per item.
2.3.1.16 Part 19 - Calibration limit high
This part is the calibrated upper limit.
2.3.1.17 Part 20 - Calibration limit low
This part is the calibrated lower limit.
2.3.1.18 Part 21 - Actual start
This part sets the geometrical value.
2.3.1.19 Part 22 - Actual stop
This part is the calculated stop value.
2.3.1.20 Part 23 - Actual restart
This part sets the geometrical value of actual restart.
2.3.1.21 Part 24 - Final value
This part sets the final geometrical value.
2.3.1.22 Part 25 - Required stop
This part is the required stop part.
2.3.1.23 Part 26 - Maximum output
This part sets the final geometrical value.
2.3.1.24 Part 27 - Maximum input
This part sets the final geometrical value.
2.3.1.25 Part 28 - Confirmed value
This part is the confirmed value part.

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
2.3.1.26 Part 38 - Coupled item
This part enables control of a particular item from a separate item enabling access to item that are
not directly accessible.
2.3.1.27 Part 39 - Coupled part
This part enables control of a particular part from a separate item enabling access to parts that are
not directly accessible.
2.3.2 Parts 100 to 199
2.3.2.1 Part 101 - F1 item type
This part enables reduction of the Item type for use by Facility 1:
0 = Rx Integer
1 = Tx Integer
2 = Rx Boolean
3 = Tx Boolean
4 = Process types.
2.3.2.2 Part 102 - F1 count
Count of the number of times this channel has been transmitted (Tx), received (Rx) or processed.
2.3.2.3 Part 103 - Physical address
This part is the physical address of an item. It comprises of three elements that can be decoded from
the value of the item:
RTU address from 0 to 5 (part 104)
Device address from 0 to 127
Read\write bit 0 or 1.
2.3.2.4 Part 104 - RTU address
This part is the address of the RTU to which the associated item points. It is part of the physical
address. Only receive and transmit items have a direct connection to the hardware, but process items
show an RTU address of 0 (ICCA-A) which is not true.
2.3.2.5 Part 105 - Device address
This part is the device address, indicating the PCB and channel with which the item is
communicating
2.3.2.6 Part 106 - Read/Write address
This is the read/write address.
Read = 1
Write = 0.
1015122 02 Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes
Page 294 Copyright ©2013 Elekta Limited. All rights reserved.

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
2.3.2.7 Part 110 - Valid value
This part is the raw value that is added to the offset part. The value of part 110 is calculated by
adding the calibration offset value part 10 to the raw data part 111.
2.3.2.8 Part 111 - Raw data
This part is the raw data read from an AI12 PCB or a DIE PCB. The raw data can be a 12-bit digital
representation of an analog value from an AI12 PCB, or a binary value that is 1 bit of a 16-bit word
from a DIE PCB. The value of part 111 is not the true value of the source data, due to 0volt
differences.
The value of part 14 distinguishes between incoming analog or digital data.
2.3.2.9 Part 112 - Required setting
This part is the required setting part, initially derived from part 1 × part 12 (inverse gain). For
process items and stand-alone transmit items, part 112 is used as a set value. For servo process
items, the value may be adjusted automatically by feedback when the servo is enabled.
2.3.2.10 Part 114 - Change raw value
This part is the accumulated error from the output of the servo process item. The part is used
mainly in transmit items as the new set value, to correct any error in the corresponding transmit
item.
2.3.2.11 Part 120 - Limit enable
When set enables Facility 1 log faults due to limit errors.
2.3.2.12 Part 121 - High limit binary
This part is uncalibrated units derived from part 19. Part 121 has the same function as part 19, but
with different units. Thus:
part 121 = part 19 × (part 12/2048) + part 10
2.3.2.13 Part 122 - Low limit binary
This part is the uncalibrated units derived from part 20. Part 122 has the same function as part 20,
but with different units. Thus:
part 122 = part 20 × (part 12/2048) + part 10
2.3.2.14 Part 123 - Absolute high limit
This part is the upper design limit. The part functions in the same way as part 121 but cannot be
adjusted.
2.3.2.15 Part 124 - Absolute low limit
This part is the lower design limit. The part functions in the same way as part 122 but cannot be
adjusted.
2.3.2.16 Part 125 - Max output
Clamps maximum value of Tx items.
Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator 1015122 02
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
2.3.2.17 Part 126 - Min output
Clamps minimum value of Tx items
2.3.2.18 Part 127 - Limit fault
Set to True (i255) if Limit fault is set otherwise false.
2.3.2.19 Part 130 - Process/filter identification
This part is the process identification part: for example, the filter type for receive items.
For items that display the value -32767 when HT is Off, the real value can be displayed by setting the
value of part 130 to 10.
2.3.2.20 Part 131 - Parameter 1 function
The function of part 131 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For example, in movement start items, part 131 is the gain value of the check potentiometer.
2.3.2.21 Part 132 - Parameter 2 function
The function of part 132 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For example, in movement start items, part 132 is the offset value of the check potentiometer.
2.3.2.22 Part 133 - Parameter 3 function
The function of part 133 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For check potentiometer items, part 133 is the amount by which the value of the check
potentiometer can vary from that of the coarse potentiometer without an error.
For steering servo items 2R and 2T, part 133 is a pointer to the lookup table that the item is using.
Thus:
2R low = 0
2T low =1
2R high = 2
2T high = 3
2R mid = 4
2T mid =5
2.3.2.23 Part 134 - Parameter 4 function
The function of part 134 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For demand items, part 134 contains the quadratic gain.
For the light field item, part 134 is the time delay before the light is switched Off.
For movement control items, part 134 is the raw value of the maximum permitted unexpected
movement.
For magnet systems, the change threshold should be 1.
2.3.2.24 Part 135 - Parameter 5 function
The function of part 135 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For demand items, part 135 contains the linear gain.
1015122 02 Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes

Item Part Values
Definitions of parts for the digital accelerator
For the magnetron tuner servo, part 135 is the change threshold used to set the value of integrated
composite phase error that must accumulate before the magnetron attempts to correct.
For magnet systems, the overshoot should be 1000.
2.3.2.25 Part 136 - Parameter 6 function
The function of part 136 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For steering servo items 2R and 2T, part 136 is the adjustment to the set value at a given gantry
angle, as specified in the lookup table.
2.3.2.26 Part 137 - Process param 7
This is the part that is available for parameter of process item which is saveable in the digital
accelerator database save.
2.3.2.27 Part 138 - Process param 8
This is the part that is available for parameter of process item which is saveable in the digital
accelerator database save.
2.3.2.28 Part 139 - Process param 9
This is the part that is available for parameter of process item which is saveable in the digital
accelerator database save.
2.3.2.29 Part 140 - Deadband
In some motor systems, part 140 is set to prevent oscillation.
In demand items, part 140 sets the dead region of the potentiometer.
2.3.2.30 Part 141 - Gain 1
The function of p141 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
In some motor systems, p141 is the positional loop gain value.
For movement start items, p141 is the coarse potentiometer gain.
2.3.2.31 Part 142 - Gain 2
The function of part 142 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For process items, part 142 is the output or loop gain (for example, calibration of beam error values,
phase gain of magnetron tuner system).
For movement start items, part 142 is the fine potentiometer gain.
2.3.2.32 Part 143 - Coarse potentiometer offset
For movement start items, part 143 is the offset value for the coarse potentiometer.
2.3.2.33 Part 144 - Fine potentiometer offset
For movement start items, part 144 is the offset value for the fine potentiometer.

2.3.2.34 Part 151 - Register part
The function of part 151 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
2.3.2.35 Part 152 - Register part
The function of part 152 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For example, in i44, part 152 shows the segment dose in 1/64 of an MU: for example, 6400 for
100MU, and the value of part 152 decrements as the dose is delivered.
2.3.2.36 Part 153 - Register part
The function of part 153 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For example, in control area power supply items part 153 records the address of the last RTU to
violate a limit.
2.3.2.37 Part 154 - Register part
The function of part 154 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
2.3.2.38 Part 155 - Register part
The function of part 155 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
2.3.2.39 Part 156 - Register part
The function of part 156 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For example, in check potentiometer items, part 156 shows the difference between the coarse and
check potentiometers. If the difference is greater than the value in part 133 (normally 250), then an
error is flagged.
The value of part 156 is updated when the new value exceeds the old value.
2.3.2.40 Part 157 - Register part
The function of part 157 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
For example, in movement start items, part 157 this is the maximum fine potentiometer error.
2.3.2.41 Part 158 - Register part
The function of part 158 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
2.3.2.42 Part 159 - Register part
The function of part 159 varies, depending on the item in which it is contained.
2.3.2.43 Part 160 - Register part
In servo items, setting the value of part 160 from 2 to 0 to 3 simulates a reboot (item changes are
implemented).
2.3.2.44 Part 161 - Auto/Manual
This part sets the item to Auto or Manual mode where:

Definition of parts for the MLCi & MLCi2
2.5 Definition of parts for the MLCi & MLCi2
This section explains the purpose of significant parts used by the software items in the
MLCi/MLCi2.
™
2.5.1 Desktop Pro™ R7.0x (applicable for Integrity R1.x)
This section lists the new parts introduced and used in software Desktop Pro™ R7.0x (for
MLCi/MLCi2) and applicable for Integrity™ R1.x.
2.5.1.1 Part 128 - Autotrack position
This part is used to determine the separation between closed leaves (in units of 0.1mm).
2.5.1.2 Part 133 - Integral gain
Gantry/collimator integral gain.
2.5.1.3 Part 141 - Feed-forward gain
Gantry/collimator feed-forward gain.
2.5.1.4 Part 142 - Proportional gain
Gantry/collimator proportional gain.
2.5.1.5 Part 143 - Gradient of (ramp up) feed forward demand
Gantry/collimator rotation gradient (of ramp up) feed-forward demand.
2.5.1.6 Part 194 - Dynamic gain
This is used to scale the speed contribution derived from the dose per loop during dynamic therapy.
2.5.1.7 Part 195 - Desired
This part returns the desired position of the diaphragm in same units as raw value (part 101).
2.5.1.8 Part 216 - Scaled speed
This part is the speed demand sent to the BLD.
2.5.1.9 Part 217 - Dose tolerance
This is used to calculate the positional tolerance to be used when checking the dynamic positioning
of the diaphragms.
2.5.1.10 Part 218 - Nominal dynamic speed demand
This is the calculated dynamic therapy leaf speed demand. Only used by old control algorithm.
1015122 02 Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes
Page 384 Copyright ©2013 Elekta Limited. All rights reserved.

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for the MLCi & MLCi2
2.5.1.11 Part 219 - Nominal dynamic speed
This is the calculated dynamic therapy leaf speed in 0.1mm at isocenter per second or nominal
leaves/gantry/collimator/ and PSS dynamic speed in units of degrees per second or mm.
2.5.1.12 Part 220 - Actual error
This gives the current actual (instantaneous) diaphragm/leaf/gantry/collimator/PSS positional
error.
2.5.1.13 Part 222 - Old control algorithm
This is used to force the software to use the old (not PID) leaf positioning control algorithm.
2.5.1.14 Part 223 - Averaged actual error
This gives the current actual averaged diaphragm/leaf positional error. Units of 0.1mm.
2.5.1.15 Part 224 - Number of samples
This defines the number of samples over which the leaf positional error is averaged.
2.5.1.16 Part 225 - Dynamic tolerance
This defines the tolerance to be used when checking the averaged positional error of
leaves/diaphragms during a dynamic step.
Minimum value = 4
Maximum value = 160
2.5.1.17 Part 226 - Sampled item
This is used as Item ID of leaf, diaphragm or the digital accelerator geometry to be sampled at
control loop frequency (for example, every 40 milliseconds).
2.5.1.18 Part 227 - Actual tolerance
This gives the actual positional tolerance being used by the control system when checking the
positional error of the diaphragm/leaf/gantry/collimator/PSS.
2.5.1.19 Part 228 - Closed tolerance
This is the tolerance to be used when checking the positional error of closed leaves.
Minimum value = 4
Maximum value = 160.
2.5.1.20 Part 229 - Dynamic low dose-rate tolerance
This is the tolerance to be used when checking the positional error of leaves. During a dynamic
delivery and the set step dose-rate is less then the low dose-rate threshold.
Minimum value = 4
Maximum value = 160.
Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator 1015122 02
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes
Copyright ©2013 Elekta Limited. All rights reserved. PPaaggee 338855

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for the MLCi & MLCi2
2.5.1.21 Part 230 - Low dose-rate threshold
This is the threshold at which dynamic delivery is considered to be a low dose-rate.
2.5.1.22 Part 231 - Max velocity
This is the maximum configured gantry/collimator/diaphragms or leaf velocity, in units of
0.1degrees/sec or 0.1mm/sec.
2.5.1.23 Part 232 - ICD (Inertia Compensation Distance)
This is the distance that must be moved before the system has overcome any inertia, in units of 0.01
degrees (for gantry/collimator) and 0.1mm (for leaves/diaphragm).
2.5.1.24 Part 233 - NDR BSU transient tolerance offset
The Normal Dose Rate (NDR) Beam Start Up (BSU) transient tolerance offset is the offset added to
the gantry/collimator/diaphragm/PSS tolerance accumulator at the start of the segment or restart
from interrupt, in units of 0.01degrees and 0.1mm (for leaves).
Default value = 100
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 900
2.5.1.25 Part 234 - NDR transient tolerance offset
This is the offset added to the gantry/collimator/diaphragm/PSS tolerance accumulator at the start
of the step or restart from pause, in units of 0.01degrees and 0.1mm (for leaves).
Default value = 50
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 450
2.5.1.26 Part 235 - NDR transient tolerance gain
This is applied to step speed delta at the start of the step or restart from pause. The result is added to
the gantry/collimator/diaphragm/PSS tolerance accumulator.
Default value = 10
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 150
2.5.1.27 Part 236 - NDR transient tolerance gradient
This is the number of units to subtract from the tolerance accumulator every eight cycles, in units of
0.01degrees (for gantry/collimator) and 0.1mm (for leaves/diaphragm).
Default value = 6
Minimum value = 1
Maximum value = 8
2.5.1.28 Part 237 - NDR transient tolerance time
This is the number of cycles before the tolerance accumulator is reduced.
Default value = 15
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 35
1015122 02 Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator
Item Part Values, Inhibits, and Exception Codes
Page 386 Copyright ©2013 Elekta Limited. All rights reserved.

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for the MLCi & MLCi2
2.5.1.29 Part 238 - NDR max instantaneous error tolerance
An instantaneous gantry/collimator positional error greater than this value will result in an
immediate PRF pause. The PRF will be re-enabled once the gantry/collimator/diaphragm/PSS is
back within tolerance. Measured in units of 0.01degrees and 0.1mm (for leaves).
Default value = 800
Minimum value = 10
Maximum value = 1000
2.5.1.30 Part 239 - LDR BSU transient tolerance offset
The Low Dose Rate (LDR) BSU transient tolerance offset is the offset added to the
gantry/diaphragm/PSS tolerance accumulator at the start of the segment or restart from interrupt,
in units of 0.01degrees and 0.1mm (for leaves).
Default value = 200
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 900
2.5.1.31 Part 240 - LDR transient tolerance offset
This is the offset added to the gantry/collimator/diaphragm/PSS tolerance accumulator at the start
of the step or restart from pause, in units of 0.01 degrees and 0.1mm (for leaves).
Default value = 100
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 450
2.5.1.32 Part 241 - LDR transient tolerance gain
This is applied to step speed delta at the start of the step or restart from pause. The result is added to
the gantry/collimator/diaphragm/PSS tolerance accumulator.
Default value = 10
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 150
2.5.1.33 Part 242 - LDR transient tolerance gradient
This is the number of units to subtract from the tolerance accumulator every eight cycles, in units of
0.01 degrees and 0.1mm.
Default value = 4
Minimum value = 1
Maximum value = 8
2.5.1.34 Part 243 - LDR transient tolerance time
This is the number of cycles before the tolerance accumulator is reduced.
Default value = 15
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 35

Definition of parts for the MLCi & MLCi2
2.5.1.35 Part 244 - LDR max instantaneous error tolerance
An instantaneous collimator/gantry positional error greater than this value will result in an
immediate PRF pause. The PRF will be re-enabled once the gantry/diaphragm/PSS is back within
tolerance. Measured in units of 0.01 degrees and 0.1mm (for leaves).
Default value = 800
Minimum value = 10
Maximum value = 1000
2.5.1.36 Part 245 - Proportional gain
For i2080-i2119 (R7.0x): This is the proportional gain contribution to leaf speed demand (from
-127 to +127).
2.5.1.37 Part 246 - Integral gain
For i2080-i2119 (R7.0x): This is the integral gain contribution to leaf speed demand (from -127 to
+127).
2.5.1.38 Part 247 - Derivative gain
For i2080-i2119 (R7.0x): This is the derivative gain contribution to leaf speed demand (from -127
to +127).
2.5.1.39 Part 248 - Feed-Forward gain
For i2080-i2119 (R7.0x): This is the feed-forward gain contribution to leaf speed demand (from
-127 to +127).

2.6 Definition of parts for Beam Modulator
This section explains the purpose of significant parts used by the software items in the Beam
Modulator™.
™ ™
2.6.1 Desktop Pro R5.1 (applicable for Integrity R1.x)
This section lists the parts introduced for software release Desktop Pro™ R5.1 and applicable for
Integrity™ R1.x.
2.6.1.1 Part 100 - Set
This part is the value used by the TCC at run time as the prescribed value. The part is also used to
hold reference values such as Item_Calibrate.
For i2000 (R5.1): Used to define the radiation type of the shape.
0 = No modality set
111 = Electron mode
222 = Xray mode.
For i2002 (R5.1): Used to define and draw the video column of the Reference Reflector being
calibrated.
For i2003 (R5.1): Used to define the shape to be loaded.
(For example, 1 = Irregular shape; 2 = Rectangular shape).
For i2004 (R5.1): Puts the Beam Modulator™ control system into the calibrate mode of the specified
item. The value of this item-part specifies the item to be calibrated:
Item_Reference_Refl_01 to Item_Reference_Refl_04
Item_Leaf_Pair_01 to Item_Leaf_Pair_40
Item_Diaphragm_X1 to Item_Diaphragm_Y2
Item_X_Head_Cntrl
Item_Y_Head_Cntrl
Item_Field_X_Autotrack_Fitted
Item_Field_Y_Autotrack_Fitted
A value of nil will clear the calibrate mode.
For i2006 (R5.1): Used to set the Beam Modulator™ control system into Diagnostic Control mode
(TRUE).
(For example, 1 = TRUE; 2 = FALSE).
For i2177 (R5.1): Holds information on the energy setting of the Beam Modulator™.
0 = Null cal. block
1 = LOW MV
2 = HIGH MV
3 = CHECK-R
4 = 3RD X-RAY
8 = HDRE 1
9 = HDRE 2
11 = 4 MeV

Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
12 = 6 MeV
13 = 8 MeV

For i2178 (R5.1): Sets the Beam Modulator™ to treatment in progress.
For i2180-i2181 (R5.1): If prescribed, must be zero. Only allowed if electron treatment prescribed.
For i2182-i2183 (R5.1): Used to define and view the prescribed/required applicator size. Only
allowed if electron treatment prescribed. In units of 0.1mm.
For i2203 (R5.1): Used to define the ID of the TCC Segment for the current shape.
For i2216 (R5.1): Selects the required power-up/power-down state of the MLC control system.
SC_NOT_INITIALISED ‘0’ –Power-down.
SC_SYSTEM_ON ‘8’ –Power-up.
2.6.1.2 Part 101 - Raw value
This part is the raw value received through the serial link. The value is obtained directly from the
beam limiting device and is converted into a format that can be read by the TCC.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R5.1): The position of the centre of the leaf’s reflector along its
video line. In units of 0.1 pixels.
For i2162 (R5.1): Defines the segment dose when the next shape is to be loaded.
For i2163 (R5.1): Used to identify the step (shape) in the current segment.
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 1999.
For i2168 (R5.1): Used to return the Accessory Mount information as read from the Beam
Modulator™ beam limiting device.
For i2169 (R5.1): Used to return the Accessory Fitment information as read from the Beam
Modulator™ beam limiting device.
For i2173-i2174 (R5.1): The applicator size read directly from the beam limiting device.
For i2191 (R5.1): The current setting of the 10V reference value as read from the beam limiting
device.
For i2192 (R5.1): The current setting of the 0V reference value as read from the beam limiting
device.
For i2198 (R5.1): The camera background level. Calculated from the raw video image when the
control system is powered up.
For i2217 (R5.1): The current setting of the camera condition value as read from the beam limiting
device.
For i2218 (R5.1): The current setting of the leaf bank positive rail value as read from the beam
limiting device.
For i2219 (R5.1): The current setting of the leaf bank negative rail value as read from the beam
limiting device.

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
2.6.1.3 Part 102 - Calibration offset
This part is used by the TCC to hold a calibration offset adjustment value, for example, the value
calculated in the calibrate leaf minor offset function.
For i2007-i2008 (R5.1): The offset (in 0.1 pixels) to be applied to the raw leaf position when
converting to a real physical position. Usually referred to as the ‘Major Offset’.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R5.1): The offset (in 0.1 pixels) to be applied to the raw leaf
position when converting to a real physical position. Usually referred to as the ‘Minor Offset’.
Specified in units of 0.1 pixels. Minimum and maximum values scaled to be equivalent of
+2.00cm and –2.00cm.
2.6.1.4 Part 103 - Calibration gain
This part is used by the TCC to hold a calibration gain adjustment value.
For i2005 (R5.1): Defines the gain to be used when converting between physical dimensions in
0.1mm and video dimensions in 0.1 pixels. Usually referred to as the ‘Major Gain’.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R5.1): The adjustment that needs to be applied to the ‘Major
Gain’ (i2005, part 103) to correct the positioning of the specific leaf. Usually referred to as the
‘Minor Gain’.
Actual Gain = Major Gain * Minor Gain / 2048. (that is, 2048 = unity).
2.6.1.5 Part 109 - Video column
This part is the calibrated column position of the item. It is used to calibrate the reference reflectors.
For i2010-i2013 (R5.1): The calibrated column position of the item.
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 767.
2.6.1.6 Part 110 - Video line
For i2010-i2013 and i2020-i2059 (R5.1): This part is the calibrated line position of the item. It is
used to calibrate the reference reflectors and the leaf video lines, and the permitted range is:
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 511.
2.6.1.7 Part 111 - Actual
This part holds the actual value for the item, calculated from the raw part.
For i2001 (R5.1): Used to report the video line currently being calibrated.
Item Video Line (2001) – Initialize Part (175)
Initializes the video screen. If True then the display will be initialized. If False the video will be
closed down.
For i2002 (R5.1): Used to report the video column currently being calibrated.
For i2004 (R5.1): Returns the item ID of the item being calibrated. Nil indicates no item being
calibrated.
For i2006 (R5.1): The actual part is normally read only. However, with this item the part is used to
write a value and enables additional software diagnostic/testing functions.
Bit 0 – Display status of the BLD enable signals as indicators at top of graphics display
Bit 4 – Emulator only, simulate Frame Grabber error

Bit 5 – Emulator only, simulate 1553 error

For i2010-i2013 (R5.1): Returns the mean pixel intensity of the reflector.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R5.1): The position of the leaf after the ‘Major’ and ‘Minor’
offset have been applied to the raw value (part 101). In units of 0.1 pixels.
For i2162 (R5.1): The current segment dose as reported to the Beam Modulator™ control system by

This part is the value used by the TCC at run time as the prescribed value. The part is also used to

converting to a real physical position. Usually referred to as the ‘Major Offset’.

position when converting to a real physical position. Usually referred to as the ‘Minor Offset’.

+2.00cm and –2.00cm.

2.6.2.4 Part 103 - Calibration gain
This part is used by the TCC to hold a calibration gain adjustment value.
For i2005 (R6.2x): Defines the gain to be used when converting between physical dimensions in
0.1mm and video dimensions in 0.1 pixels. Usually referred to as the ‘Major Gain’.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The adjustment that needs to be applied to the ‘Major
Gain’ (i2005, part 103) to correct the positioning of the specific leaf. Usually referred to as the
‘Minor Gain’.
Actual Gain = Major Gain * Minor Gain / 2048. (that is, 2048 = unity).
For i2232 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Proportional gain used in new PID control algorithm. In
units of 0.01.
For i2233 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Integral gain used in new PID control algorithm. In units of
0.01.
For i2234 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Derivative gain used in new PID control algorithm. In units
of 0.01.
For i2235 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Feed-forward gain used in new PID control algorithm. In
units of 0.01.

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
2.6.2.12 Part 120 - Tolerance
For i2009 (R6.2x): This part sets the leaf position tolerance (in units of 0.1 pixel) within the range:
Initial value = 8
Minimum value = 4
Maximum value = 160.
For i2009, i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The tolerance to be used when checking the averaged
positional error of diaphragms during a static step. Units of 0.1mm.
Minimum value = 1.0mm
Maximum value = 10.0mm.
2.6.2.13 Part 121 - Deadband
This part instructs the TCC if movement is still required on a particular item.
The range is:
Initial = 6
Minimum value = 2
Maximum value = 160.
For i2009 (R7.0x): Instructs the system if movement is still required on a particular leaf. In units of
0.1mm.
2.6.2.14 Part 122 - Maximum speed
For i2009 (R6.2x): This part sets the maximum allowable speed for an item when trying to reach
position. The range is:
Minimum value = -127
Maximum value = 127.
2.6.2.15 Part 123 - Separation
For i2009 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This part sets the minimum possible separation between one
leaf and an immediately adjacent leaf. In units of 0.1mm. The range is:
Initial = 12
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 100
Factory standard = 50.
2.6.2.16 Part 127 - Deceleration
This part is used to determine the slow-down rate of the item in calculations of leaf and diaphragm
movement. The range is:
Initial = 5500
Minimum value = 1000
Maximum value = 9999.
For i2009 (R6.2x): Used in the calculation for leaf and diaphragm movement to determine the slow
down rate of that item.
2.6.2.17 Part 128 - Autotrack position
For i2009 (Integrity™ R1.x): This part is used to determine the separation between closed leaves (in
units of 0.1mm).

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
2.6.2.18 Part 129 - Scaled actual
This part is the quotient value of the actual and gain values for a diaphragm.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The position of the leaf after the ‘Major’ and ‘Minor’
gain have been applied to the actual value (part 111). In units of 0.1mm.
For i2060-i2063 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Returns the X/Y positions of the fixed diaphragm in
units of 0.1mm.
For i2224 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The current gantry position. In units of 0.1 degrees.
For i2225 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The current collimator position. In units of 0.1 degrees.
For i2226 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The current PSS isocenter rotation position. In units of 0.1
degrees.
For i2227 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The current PSS longitude position. In units of 0.1cm.
For i2228 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The current PSS lateral position. In units of 0.1cm.
For i2229 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The current PSS height position. In units of 0.1cm.
2.6.2.19 Part 133 - Integral gain
For i71, i76 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Gantry/collimator rotation integral gain.
2.6.2.20 Part 141 - Feed-forward gain
For i71, i76 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Gantry/collimator rotation feed-forward gain.
2.6.2.21 Part 142 - Proportional gain
For i71, i76 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Gantry/collimator rotation proportional gain.
2.6.2.22 Part 143 - Gradient (of ramp up) feed forward demand
For i71, i76 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Gantry/collimator rotation gradient (of ramp up)
feed-forward demand.
2.6.2.23 Part 171 - X overlap
For i2009 (R6.2x): The value of part 171 defines the proximity of the diaphragms to the top of the
outermost leaf of a field before an extra leaf is opened (in increments of 0.1mm). The range is:
Initial = 10
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 50.
2.6.2.24 Part 172 - Leaf reflector width/set reflector width
For i2007-i2008 (R6.2x): This item-part is used to perform a block write to all the excepted leaf
reflector width item-parts in one leaf bank, for example, writing to i2007 part 172 will set i2080 to
i2119 part 172 to the supplied value.
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): Defines, in pixels, the expected width of the reference reflector.
Minimum value = 1
Maximum value = 50.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): Defines, in pixels, the expected width of the leaf
reflector.

Maximum value = 50.
2.6.2.25 Part 173 - Set reflector height
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): Defines, in pixels, the expected height of the reference reflector.
Minimum value = 1
Maximum value = 50.
2.6.2.26 Part 175 - Initialise
For i2001 (R6.2x): Initializes the video screen. If “True” then the display will be initialized. If “False”
then the video will be closed down.
2.6.2.27 Part 176 - Actual pixel count X
For i2010-i2013 and i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The number of pixels with intensity
above the threshold found in the horizontal search window.
2.6.2.28 Part 177 - Actual pixel count Y
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): The number of pixels with intensity above the threshold found in the
vertical search window.
2.6.2.29 Part 178 - Actual video column
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): The actual column position (in 0.1 pixels) of the reference reflector.
2.6.2.30 Part 179 - Display calibration line
For i2020-i2059 (R6.2x): This item-part acts as a command to the Beam Modulator™-CS, for
example, there is no storage associated with it. It displays a single video line for all leaf pairs.
2.6.2.31 Part 180 - Delete calibration line
For i2020-i2059 (R6.2x): This item-part acts as a command to the Beam Modulator™ -CS, for
example, there is no storage associated with it. It removes all leaf pair video lines.
2.6.2.32 Part 181 - Intensity threshold
For i2009 (R6.2x): Used to define the Reference Reflector mean intensity warning threshold.
Warnings will be raised for reflectors with a mean intensity this threshold. The pixel intensity which
the reference reflector pixels are considered “non-black”.
For i2256-i2271 (R6.2x): The intensity threshold to be used in the associated zone.
2.6.2.33 Part 182 - Actual video line
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): The actual line position (in 0.1 pixels) of the reference reflector item.
For i2020-i2059 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Reports actual video line being used for reflector
search. Lines used will vary as system compensations for “Video Line Shift” (VLS). Read only.
2.6.2.34 Part 183 - Pixel count minimum threshold
For i2009 (R6.2x): Minimum percentage of pixels in search rectangle for valid reflector.

Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
For i2009, i2014, i2020-i2059, i2060-i2063, i2080-i2119, i2120-i2159 and i2237, i2224-i2229
(R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): General purpose registers used by control system during software
development.
2.6.2.43 Part 192 - Average reflector width
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): The averaged width of the reflector.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The averaged width of the leaf’s reflector.
2.6.2.44 Part 193 - Average reflector height
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): The averaged height of the reflector.
2.6.2.45 Part 196 - Scaled desired
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The desired position of leaf in same units as the actual
part (part 111) during dynamic therapy.
For i2060-i2063 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Returns the X/Y position of the fixed diaphragm in
units of 0.1mm.
For i2224 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The desired gantry position. In units of 0.1 degrees.
For i2225 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The desired collimator rotation position. In units of 0.1
degrees.
For i2226 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The desired PSS isocenter rotation position. In units of 0.1
degrees.
For i2227 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The desired PSS longitude position. In units of 0.1cm.
For i2228 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The desired PSS lateral position. In units of 0.1cm.
For i2229 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The desired PSS height position. In units of 0.1cm.
2.6.2.46 Part 197 - Leaf cycle count LSW
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): Used with leaf cycle count MSW to record number of
times leaf motor has been turned on.
2.6.2.47 Part 198 - Leaf cycle count MSW
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): Used with leaf cycle count LSW to record number of
times leaf motor has been turned on. Each unit corresponds to 65536 counts.
2.6.2.48 Part 200 - Count
For i2009 (R6.2x): Counts the number of state transitions from all reflectors found to one or more
reflectors lost. The counters are read from and written to the Beam Modulator™-CS database when
the database is loaded and saved respectively. The counter can be reset by writing to the item-part.
For i2010-i2013 (R6.2x): A count of the number of times the reflector has changed state from
‘found’ to ‘not found’.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): A count of the number of times the reflector has
changed state from ‘found’ to ‘not found’. The counters are reset to zero each time the system is
booted. The counters can be reset by writing to the item-part.

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
2.6.2.49 Part 201 - Pixel count minimum
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The minimum number of non-black pixels found in the
associated leaf reflector since the system booted.
2.6.2.50 Part 202 - Pixel count maximum
For i2080 - i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): The maximum number of non-black pixels found in
the associated leaf reflector since the system booted.
2.6.2.51 Part 203 - Slow down distance
For i2009 (R6.2x): The distance (in units of 0.1mm) from the desired leaf position at which the
deceleration logic is applied.
2.6.2.52 Part 204 - Slow down maximum speed
For i2009 (R6.2x): The maximum speed demand that can be applied within the slow down distance
(see part 203).
2.6.2.53 Part 205 - Movement delta
For i2009 (R6.2x): Used to reset all leaf lateral movement values to specified value (usually zero).
See i2080 - i2159, part 205.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R6.2x): Used to record the magnitude of the lateral movement
of the leaf.
2.6.2.54 Part 206 - Leaf motor current
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (Integrity™ R1.x): Used to record the current taken by leaf drive
motor.
2.6.2.55 Part 207 - Leaf max current
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (Integrity™ R1.x): Used to record the maximum current taken by
leaf drive motor.
2.6.2.56 Part 215 - Size warning count
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Used to record the number of times the associated leaf
reflector size has crossed the min/max warning threshold.
2.6.2.57 Part 221 - Reset thresholds
For i2009 (R6.2x): The pixel intensity value to be set in all 16 zone thresholds. A read of this value
will always return nil.
Minimum value = 164
Maximum value = 254.

Item Part Values
Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
™ ™
2.6.3 Desktop Pro R7.0x (applicable for Integrity R1.x)
In addition to parts listed in Section2.6.2. This section lists the new parts introduced and used in
software Desktop Pro™ R7.0x for the first time (for Beam Modulator™) and also applicable for
Integrity™ R1.x.
2.6.3.1 Part 107 - Coarse gain
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Individual leaf deceleration value.
2.6.3.2 Part 194 - Dynamic gain
For i2009 (R7.0x): Used to scale the speed contribution derived from the dose per loop during
dynamic therapy.
2.6.3.3 Part 195 - Desired
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Returns the desired position of the diaphragm in same
units as raw value (part 101) during dynamic therapy.
2.6.3.4 Part 209 - Sensor activated
For i2190 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Value “0” indicates that i2190 sensor has been activated.
Value “1” indicates that sensor has not been activated.
2.6.3.5 Part 215 - Size warning count
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Used to simulate positional errors. Value is in units of
0.1mm and defines the simulated error between the desired and actual position. A value of nil
indicates that no error is required.
2.6.3.6 Part 216 - Scaled speed
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Speed demand sent to the BLD.
Minimum value = -127
Maximum value = +127.
2.6.3.7 Part 217 - Dose tolerance
For i2009 (R7.0x): Used to calculate the positional tolerance to be used when checking the dynamic
positioning of the leaves/diaphragms.
2.6.3.8 Part 218 - Nominal dynamic speed demand
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Calculated dynamic therapy leaf speed demand. Only
used by old control algorithm.
2.6.3.9 Part 219 - Nominal dynamic speed
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Calculated dynamic therapy leaf speed in 0.1mm per
second.
For i2224 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Gantry nominal dynamic speed in 0.1degrees per second.
For i2225 (R7.0x): Collimator rotation nominal dynamic speed in 0.1degrees per second.

For i2226 (R7.0x): PSS isocenter rotation nominal dynamic speed in 0.1degrees per second.
For i2227 (R7.0x): PSS longitude nominal dynamic speed in 0.1cm per second.
For i2228 (R7.0x): The PSS lateral nominal dynamic speed. In units of 0.1cm per second.
2.6.3.10 Part 220 - Actual error
For i2060-i2063 (R7.0x): Actual (instantaneous) positional error for diaphragm.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Current actual leaf positional error.
For i2224 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The gantry positional error. In units of 0.1 degrees.
For i2225 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The collimator rotation positional error. In units of 0.1
degrees.
For i2226 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS isocenter rotation positional error. In units of 0.1
degrees.
For i2227 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS longitude positional error. In units of 0.1cm.
For i2228 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS lateral positional error. In units of 0.1cm.
For i2229 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS height positional error. In units of 0.1cm.
2.6.3.11 Part 222 - Old control algorithm
For i2009 (R7.0x): Used to force the software to use the old (not PID) leaf positioning control
algorithm.
2.6.3.12 Part 223 - Averaged actual error
For i2060-i2063 (R7.0x): Current actual averaged diaphragm positional error. Units of 0.1mm.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Gives the current actual averaged leaf positional error in
units of 0.1mm.
2.6.3.13 Part 224 - Number of samples
For i2009 (R7.0x): Defines the number of samples over which the leaf positional error is averaged.
For i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Defines the number of samples over which the diaphragm
positional error is averaged.
2.6.3.14 Part 225 - Dynamic tolerance
For i2009 (R7.0x): The tolerance to be used when checking the averaged positional error of leaves
during a dynamic step.
Minimum value = 4
Maximum value = 160.
For i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The tolerance to be used when checking the averaged
positional error of diaphragms during a dynamic step. Units of 0.1mm.
Minimum value = 1.0mm
Maximum value = 10.0mm.
2.6.3.15 Part 226 - Sampled item
For i2006 (R7.0x): Item ID of leaf, diaphragm or the digital accelerator geometry to be sampled at
control loop frequency (for example, every 40 milliseconds).

2.6.3.16 Part 227 - Actual tolerance
For i2060-i2063 (R7.0x): Actual tolerance being used by the control system when checking the
positional error of the diaphragm. In units of 0.1mm.
For i2080-i2119 and i2120-i2159 (R7.0x): Actual tolerance being used by the control system when
checking the positional error of the leaf.
For i2224 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The gantry positional tolerance. In units of 0.1 degrees.
For i2225 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The collimator rotation positional tolerance. In units of 0.1

For i2226 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS isocenter rotation positional tolerance. In units of
0.1 degrees.
For i2227 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS longitude positional tolerance. In units of 0.1cm.

For i2229 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): The PSS height positional tolerance. In units of 0.1cm.
2.6.3.17 Part 228 - Closed tolerance
For i2009 (R7.0x): The tolerance to be used when checking the positional error of closed leaves.
Minimum value = 4
Maximum value = 160.
2.6.3.18 Part 230 - Low dose-rate threshold
For i2230 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): Dose rates below this value trigger selection of low dose rate

2.6.3.19 Part 231 - Max. velocity
For i2014 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This is the maximum configured collimator/gantry velocity,

2.6.3.20 Part 232 - ICD (Inertia Compensation Distance)

Default value = 100
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 900

Definition of parts for Beam Modulator™
2.6.3.22 Part 234 - NDR transient tolerance offset
For i2009, i2014-i2019 and i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This is the offset added to the
collimator/gantry/PSS tolerance accumulator at the start of the step or restart from pause, in units of
0.01degrees and 0.1mm.
Default value = 50
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 450
2.6.3.23 Part 235 - NDR transient tolerance gain
For i2009, i2014-i2019 and i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This is applied to the step speed delta
at the start of the step or restart from pause. The result is added to the collimator/gantry/PSS
tolerance accumulator.
Default value = 10
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 150
2.6.3.24 Part 236 - NDR transient tolerance gradient
For i2009, i2014-i2019 and i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This is the number of units to
subtract from the tolerance accumulator every eight cycles, in units of 0.01degrees and 0.1mm.
Default value = 6
Minimum value = 1
Maximum value = 8
2.6.3.25 Part 237 - NDR transient tolerance time
For i2009, i2014-i2019 and i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This is the number of cycles before
the tolerance accumulator is reduced.
Default value = 15
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 35
2.6.3.26 Part 238 - NDR max instantaneous error tolerance
For i2009, i2014-i2019 and i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): An instantaneous positional error
greater than this value will result in an immediate PRF pause. The PRF will be re-enabled once the
collimator/gantry/PSS is back within tolerance. Measured in units of 0.01 degrees and 0.1mm.
Default value = 800
Minimum value = 10
Maximum value = 1000
2.6.3.27 Part 239 - LDR BSU transient tolerance offset
For i2009, i2015-i2019 and i2237 (R7.0x and Integrity™ R1.x): This is the offset added to the
collimator/gantry/PSS tolerance accumulator at the start of the segment or restart from interrupt, in
units of 0.01degrees and 0.1mm.
Default value = 200
Minimum value = 0
Maximum value = 900

Introduction
B.1 Introduction
This chapter gives a list of Linac calibration files and 2R and 2T Look-Up tables applicable to Service
Mode for the Elekta Digital Linear Accelerator. For more information, refer to the Elekta Digital
Accelerator Corrective Maintenance Manual.

(except the 2R, 2T and hand controller files, which contain look up tables).

Linac calibration files and 2R and 2T Look-Up tables
2R and 2T Look-Up tables
B.3 2R and 2T Look-Up tables
These tables show the coil correction currents to the 2R and 2T set currents for different gantry
angles.
The tables are contained in the following files.
Be5510 is the 2R low power look-up table
Be5515 is the 2R high power look-up table
Be5517 is the 2R mid power look-up table
Be5511 is the 2T low power look-up table
Be5516 is the 2T high power look-up table
Be5518 is the 2T mid power look-up table
The look-up tables are also contained in special Items with each position in the table being a part of
the Item; the Items are as follows.
Item 1190 2R low power
Item 1191 2T low power
Item 1195 2R high power
Item 1196 2T high power
Item 1197 2R mid power
Item 1198 2T mid power
The Items can be viewed or edited with the use of the Edit Lookup Tables.
